commit 656cb0450aeb73b25d7d26980af342b37ac4c568
Author:     Jiasheng Jiang <jiasheng@iscas.ac.cn>
AuthorDate: Tue Feb 15 17:58:08 2022 +0800
Commit:     Peter Ross <pross@xvid.org>
CommitDate: Fri Feb 18 16:54:11 2022 +1100

    avcodec/vp3: Add missing check for av_malloc
    
    Since the av_malloc() may fail and return NULL pointer,
    it is needed that the 's->edge_emu_buffer' should be checked
    whether the new allocation is success.
    
    Fixes: d14723861b ("VP3: fix decoding of vid

content.snippet:
commit 656cb0450aeb73b25d7d26980af342b37ac4c568
Author: Jiasheng Jiang <jiasheng@iscas.ac.cn>
Date:   Tue Feb 15 17:58:08 2022 +0800

    avcodec/vp3: Add missing check for av_malloc
    
    Since the av_malloc() may fail and return NULL pointer,
    it is needed that the 's->edge_emu_buffer' should be checked
    whether the new allocation is success.
    
    Fixes: d14723861b ("VP3: fix decoding of videos with stride > 2048")
    Reviewed-by: Peter Ross <pross@xvid.org>
    Signed-off-by: Jiasheng Jiang <jiasheng@iscas.ac.cn>

 libavcodec/vp3.c | 7 ++++++-
 1 file changed, 6 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

content.claim: git log --follow around changed path libavcodec/vp3.c
content.snippet:
656cb0450a avcodec/vp3: Add missing check for av_malloc
f025b8e110 avcodec/threadframe: Add ff_thread_(get|release)_ext_buffer()
b3551b6072 avcodec/thread: Move ff_thread_(await|report)_progress to new header
46309f262c avcodec/vp3: Don't output bogus warning
96caa01f13 avcodec/vp3: Check version in all cases when VP4 code is not built

content.description: The return value of av_malloc() is stored directly in s->edge_emu_buffer without any subsequent NULL check; control flows to later code that dereferences s->edge_emu_buffer as a valid buffer pointer.
After av_malloc(), the code checks if s->edge_emu_buffer is NULL and, if so, sets ret = AVERROR(ENOMEM) and jumps to the error cleanup label, preventing any use of the NULL pointer.

The condition 'if (!s->edge_emu_buffer)' guards the allocation block so av_malloc() is only called when the buffer has not yet been allocated. However, this guard only checks pre-existing NULL state and does not protect against allocation failure.

Before the fix, there is no condition checking whether s->edge_emu_buffer became NULL after the av_malloc() call — i.e., the vulnerable code lacks 'if (!s->edge_emu_buffer) { ret = AVERROR(ENOMEM); goto error; }' following the allocation.

content.summary: Missing NULL check on av_malloc() return value in vp3_decode_frame() — the caller dereferences s->edge_emu_buffer without verifying the allocation succeeded, yielding a NULL pointer dereference on memory exhaustion.
content.mechanism: In vp3_decode_frame(), when s->edge_emu_buffer is NULL (first frame or reinit), the code calls av_malloc(9 * FFABS(s->current_frame.f->linesize[0])) and stores the result directly into s->edge_emu_buffer. The original code used a bare if-without-braces (only one statement after the if), which allocated but never checked the return value. If av_malloc() returned NULL (OOM), the NULL pointer would be stored and later used as a buffer, causing a NULL pointer dereference crash (CWE-476). The fix wraps the allocation in braces and inserts an explicit NULL check that returns AVERROR(ENOMEM) via the error path when allocation fails.

content.description: The original code used a single-line if-without-braces for the guard and allocation, which is a syntactic pattern prone to missing error-handling insertions; the fix changed it to a block-scoped if with braces. This coding-style risk contributed to the missing NULL check.

content.reason: The git observation snippet for the unified diff is truncated and does not show the full 80-line context, so downstream code paths that dereference s->edge_emu_buffer are inferred from the function signature and CWE-476 context rather than directly observed.
